steps.title: Use point slope form
steps.content:
The line has slope $-\frac{4}{3}$ and passes through $(-9,0)$.
$$y-0=-\frac{4}{3}(x-(-9))$$
$$y=-\frac{4}{3}(x+9)$$

steps.title: Find the vertical intercept
steps.content: Set $x=0$ to find where the line crosses the vertical axis. $$y=-\frac{4}{3}(0+9)$$ $$y=-\frac{36}{3}=-12$$ The answer is -12.
